messages.reply_html:
andykirby
Main advantages of narrow are:
  • Narrower bandwidth so it can avoid interference.
  • Virtually the same performance as current settings but faster and in some cases more reliable. (weak links often improved) Reduces collisions.
  • Allows for 4 channels in the EU allocation.
  • Reduces perceptive noise floor. This is subjective.
  • This has been tried and tested in difficult areas like USA (die hard seattle mesh) and Bratislava with very bad RF interference on the center frequency of 869.525
 (edited)

messages.author: Mopatops (Bristol)
messages.text: I agree, there should be options. The trouble is, it's changing a standard that has already been set. I know quite a few people who were put off when we changed from the 867Mhz freq to 869.525 (had to be done), then from SF10 to 11, now another change. Some aren't into changing settings, and just want to plug it in and play. 250khz is working fine at the moment on our mesh around Bristol & Midlands, and if interference starts, it could well be on one of the 4 frequencies chosen for the 62.5khz anyway. Yes, we could move around to get away, but it could pop up again somewhere else. It's never going to be perfect, because we share the band with so many others, but one thing changing to 62.5khz will cause is the loss of collision detection with Meshtastic, and that will give people grief in some areas. Maybe it should just be a local thing for areas suffering from interference on a specific area of the band, and not necessarily be pushed to everyone.

messages.author: recrof
messages.text: has more thought been put into this other than lower noise floor you must have seen that we moved the frequency to the edge - that is huge help in avoiding interference. even when the signal is very week as you have a very long window to decode it. Doesn't making the signal narrower actually reduce the likelyhood of a decode as it could be completely wiped out by interference operating in the same part of the spectrum (whilst wider would give the receive more time to decode each bit?) not if the signal gets blown up by something stronger - AGC on the receiver will need to shut - in that case it won't hear you at all. in the reality, you want to rx/tx on the quietest place as possible.

@andykirby There is a bigger problem for me at least (and possibly others) - and that is that the move to Narrow seems to have exposed a problem with incompatibility between sx1276 and sx1262 devices again (probably back to the old preamble problem). Whilst this isn't a problem for most people who are splashing out on new devices like there's no tomorrow, there are us old stalwarts who are still using older Heltec V2s for Room Servers and Companions - and this move to Narrow has completely broken things for those devices. Switching back to UK Long and they work perfectly again, but on Narrow they won't talk. Really quite annoying. I do have 1 HeltecV2 which is working on Narrow - so I suspect there is a persistent Radio setting that isn't getting set properly when doing a clean flash. My suspicion is that going back to 1.7.0 ( preamble 8) and then updating incrementally to 1.8.1 may resolve the issue as this may poke the persistent setting in the Radio - I will test that tomorrow. Bloody annoying none-the-less... (edited)

messages.author: Liam Cottle
messages.text: Someone will have to open an RFC on the GitHub issues, similar to the previous one asking the community to remove the old USA presets. Unfortunately because the presets have been in the list for so long, it's possible people are actively using the settings in decent sized meshes we don't know about. Ideally, we give notification, with a banner in the app, pointing to the RFC saying it might be removed, with a poll on if we should remove it. It's easy to delete the preset from the list in 2 seconds, but I'd rather not yank things out like that.
messages.content_html: Someone will have to open an RFC on the GitHub issues, similar to the previous one asking the community to remove the old USA presets. messages.author: jeg1972 (Dunston/LobleyHill Rpt)
messages.text: I'm not advocating removing the option for wide band, I don't want to remove choice, I just want to give people the best chance of actually adopting Meshcore successfully. I'm seeing lots of Repeaters who have just used the default Wide setting, and the result is, that I can't connect to anyone North of me, they don't know any different... they used the defaults... they will hang around for a bit, nobody will connect to them so they will get disillusioned and leave... that is not how you build a community/network.
